name: Firecrawl MCP Server
description: Official Firecrawl MCP server giving AI models web scraping, crawling, site mapping, web search, and structured extraction capabilities. Available as an npm package (firecrawl-mcp) over stdio or as a hosted remote server authenticated with a firecrawl.dev API key.

evidence.value: Mature hosted API backing all MCP tools, with documented status reporting and a widely used production service

api_reliability.methodology: API stability and service maturity analysis

evidence.value: Scrape and batch scrape tools handle JavaScript-rendered pages, anti-bot mitigation, and multiple output formats; some heavily protected sites still fail

scrape_success_rate.methodology: Scrape success testing across diverse site types

evidence.value: Crawl and map tools discover most site pages with configurable depth and limits; very large or dynamic sites may be partially covered

crawl_completeness.methodology: Crawl coverage assessment against known site structures

evidence.value: Built-in automatic retries with exponential backoff for rate-limited requests and credit usage monitoring

rate_limit_handling.methodology: Rate limiting behavior review from source and docs

evidence.value: Errors surfaced as structured tool results; retry logic covers transient failures, though long-running crawls can require manual restart
Authenticates with a firecrawl.dev API key via environment variable (stdio) or the hosted remote endpoint
All fetched web content is untrusted; malicious pages can carry indirect prompt injection payloads that the consuming agent must defend against

prompt_injection_exposure.methodology: Threat modeling of untrusted web content returned to the model

evidence.value: Model-supplied URLs are fetched server-side; SSRF-style requests against internal or private endpoints are a documented risk class for fetch/scrape MCP servers

ssrf_protection.methodology: SSRF risk analysis of URL-driven tool inputs

evidence.value: API key stored in client configuration or environment; key grants full account credit usage if leaked, so it must be protected

credential_handling.methodology: API key storage and exposure analysis

evidence.value: Tools are read-oriented (scrape, crawl, search, extract) so no destructive write actions exist, but the agent can crawl arbitrary sites and consume account credits

unauthorized_action_risk.methodology: Capability and blast radius assessment of exposed tools

evidence.value: Scraped URLs and page content flow through Firecrawl's cloud service and are then returned to the LLM provider context

data_exposure.methodology: Data flow analysis of scrape and crawl pipelines

evidence.value: No built-in PII or secret filtering on scraped content; anything reachable at a URL can land in the model context

sensitive_data_protection.methodology: Privacy controls assessment of returned content

evidence.value: Request URLs and scraped content processed by Firecrawl per its privacy policy; results are additionally shared with the chosen LLM provider
Commercial terms published; users remain responsible for robots.txt, copyright, and lawful-scraping compliance for targets they crawl
Detailed setup guides for major MCP clients, tool-by-tool reference, and configuration options
Account dashboard shows request activity and credit usage; MCP tool calls are visible in client logs
MIT-licensed open source server with 6,538 GitHub stars; backend scraping service is partially proprietary
Tool set clearly enumerated: scrape, batch scrape, crawl, map, web search, and structured extract
Single npx command with one API key environment variable, or zero-install hosted remote endpoint
Single-page scrapes typically complete in seconds; full crawls of large sites are long-running asynchronous jobs
Public status page with historically high availability for the hosted API
Covers the full web data lifecycle: discovery (map, search), retrieval (scrape, batch, crawl), and structured extraction
6,538 GitHub stars and listing in major MCP client directories indicate strong adoption

strengths: Full web data toolkit: scrape, batch scrape, crawl, map, search, and structured extract
Handles JavaScript-heavy sites and returns clean LLM-ready markdown
Automatic retries with exponential backoff and credit usage monitoring
Simple setup via npx package or hosted remote server
MIT-licensed open source server with strong community adoption (6,538 stars)
Read-only tool surface with no destructive write actions
limitations: All fetched web content is untrusted and can carry indirect prompt injection payloads
Model-supplied URLs create SSRF-style risk against internal or private endpoints
Leaked API key allows arbitrary credit consumption on the account
No built-in PII or secret filtering on scraped content
Scraped data transits Firecrawl's cloud before reaching the model
Heavily bot-protected sites can still fail or return partial content

research-assistant.notes: Excellent for gathering, crawling, and extracting web sources during research workflows

data-analysis.notes: Structured extract and batch scrape turn web pages into analyzable datasets

content-creation.notes: Strong for sourcing reference material, competitor pages, and up-to-date facts for content

code-generation.notes: Useful for pulling live documentation and examples into coding sessions

customer-support.notes: Can crawl knowledge bases and product pages to ground support answers

financial-analysis.notes: Good for collecting public filings and market pages, but verify accuracy of scraped figures

legal-compliance.notes: Scraping legality and content reliability require careful human review for legal work

education.notes: Helpful for building course material from live web sources with proper attribution

best_for: Research agents that need reliable, structured web content retrieval
Teams converting websites into LLM-ready markdown or structured data
AI applications requiring crawl, search, and extraction in one server
Developers wanting a drop-in web data layer for MCP clients
